title: vicia venosa subsp. venosa
intro: Vicia venosa subsp. venosa, also known as the 'red vetch', is a perennial legume plant native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Vicia venosa subsp. venosa include its ability to reduce fever, soothe coughs, and ease digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Vicia venosa subsp. venosa can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and can be consumed as a tea or added to topical creams for skin and wound care.
